essence: Troubleshooting whether an MVM issue stems from running Claude/Codex in the wrong working folder, and whether the session ID can be grabbed to resume from a new terminal in a different directory.

essence: David quizzes the agent on extending his skill for more processing steps, running IME against the brain folder by path, committing files, deleting the Mochaccino folder, and what exactly to check during manual testing.

David decides OMI-fetch output should be processed daily on the Mac with off-the-shelf schedulers (Claude desktop scheduled tasks, claude -p, or the Agent SDK) rather than bespoke tooling, and asks for a simple three-slide hand-drawn soft-pencil deck adding OMI and OMI scheduling to the roadmap list.

essence: Directive that sync-technology API calls support create-or-update and that plugin changes and second-brain knowledge are always committed and pushed so everything stays deployed and current.

David explores the relay skill end-to-end — creating relays for AngelEye, FliHub and Lars, whether Syncthing registration happens automatically, and folder ID vs label conventions — to understand what a relay actually yields before rolling it out.

Hands-on relay skill run surfaces concrete gaps — no open-Syncthing command, no folder path/ID listing, unsafe behavior when folders already exist, missing internal/public folder patterns — with two goals set for the day: Dropbox external agent folder and skills installed on the other system.

actions.text: Update the relay skill via skill-creator: relay info/help command, full folder paths plus Syncthing IDs, non-destructive handling of existing folders (disconnect Syncthing share first), folder patterns across internal/public privacy types
Set up the Dropbox-based external agent communication folder today
Get the relay skills installed on the other system today

essence: David audits an unintended automation: after Bob's BMAD validation run the agent auto-launched Amelia's development phase without human confirmation, so he demands verification of whether validation findings were actually implemented plus conversational skill-based testing instead of remembered scripts.

actions.text: Provide conversational skill-based testing instructions, or come back with a Q&A if the right skills don't exist yet
Verify whether Bob's validation findings should have been implemented and whether Amelia addressed them

essence: UI decision: render the agent lifecycle chain only at the top of the message while loading, with an arrow marking the current agent, so users can scroll up to see what's next while always knowing where they are.

actions.text: Show lifecycle chain only at top of loading message with current-agent arrow

essence: Key operational insight: Syncthing folders must be explicitly registered and shares disabled on every machine before renames/moves or the external service will resync and corrupt data — unlike Dropbox — prompting a structured iCloud vs Dropbox vs Syncthing comparison.

actions.text: Create a reusable ChatGPT prompt comparing iCloud, Dropbox and the current Syncthing setup

essence: Relay folder convention settled: relay/{type}/{name} with the type (app/project/people) explicit in the path, to-<agent> naming for harness targets (to-paperclip, to-hermes), people folders as david-jan/david-mary/david-lars, and Syncthing for internal versus Dropbox for external parties like Lars.

actions.text: Once folder and relay conventions are settled, update the relay skills to match
